text.title: rag-eval-retrieval-metrics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rag-eval-retrieval-metrics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Passing k different from the actual retrieval top-K when calling precision_at_k.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: precision_at_k divides by k, not by number of retrieved docs. If you retrieve top-10 but pass k=5, you measure a different cut than what the retriever actually returned, producing misleading results.

failure-mode.description: Computing macro-average MRR without distinguishing MRR=0.0 (no relevant doc retrieved) from low-but-positive MRR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: mean_reciprocal_rank returns 0.0 when no relevant doc appears in the result list. Averaging this with true MRR scores conflates two different failure modes: missing relevance entirely vs. poor rank position.

failure-mode.description: Using NDCG@K with binary labels directly from a relevant set without converting to graded scores.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: NDCG@K requires graded relevance scores (float dict). With binary labels, construct relevance_scores = {doc_id: 1.0 for doc_id in relevant} — this converts NDCG to equivalent of average precision and is explicit about the binary assumption.
